title: 7 Herbal Essential Oils For Numbness In Hands
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils can be a great way to relieve numbness in your hands. These oils are made from plants and are known for their healing properties. One example is Eucalyptus globulus, which helps to improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the hands. This can be very helpful if you've been experiencing numbness due to poor circulation or injury.  Another example is Rosmarinus officinalis, also known as rosemary essential oil, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce numbness and pain in the hands. It's also great for improving memory and concentration, which can be beneficial if you've been experiencing numbness due to a condition like carpal tunnel syndrome.  Mentha x piperita, or peppermint essential oil, is another great option for relieving numbness in the hands. It helps to improve circulation and reduce inflammation, which can be very helpful if you've been experiencing numbness due to poor circulation or injury. Peppermint oil also has a cooling effect, which can be very soothing for numb hands.  Herbal teas made from these herbs can also be very helpful in relieving numbness in the hands. Drinking a warm tea made from Eucalyptus globulus, Rosmarinus officinalis, or Mentha x piperita can help to improve circulation, reduce inflammation, and promote relaxation. This can be very beneficial for people who experience numbness due to stress, anxiety, or poor sleep.  Using herbal essential oils and teas can bring many benefits to your life. By relieving numbness in your hands, you can improve your ability to perform daily tasks and activities. You can also reduce your risk of injury and improve your overall sense of well-being. Additionally, these herbs can help to promote relaxation and reduce stress, which can be beneficial for both physical and mental health.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for numbness in hands

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains compounds like eucalyptol and bornyl acetate. These components have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help reduce swelling and ease muscle tension in the hands. The cooling effect of eucalyptol can also help numbness by increasing blood flow and reducing pain. Additionally, the antioxidant properties of the essential oil's polyphenol content, such as 1,8-cineole and limonene, may help protect nerve cells from damage. By reducing inflammation and protecting nerve cells, eucalyptus globulus essential oils can help alleviate numbness in the hands.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 1 cup of water in a large bowl.
Add 2 tablespoons of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) to the bowl.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler or a microwave-safe bowl for 2-3 minutes.
Steep the Eucalyptus leaves in the mixture for 2 hours or overnight, then strain the leaves.
Bottle the essential oil and store it in a cool, dark place for later use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains rosinic acid, bornyl acetate, and camphor as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help to reduce numbness and tingling sensations in the hands. Camphor, in particular, has a vasodilating effect, which increases blood flow to the affected areas and helps to relieve numbness. The essential oil's antispasmodic properties also help to relax muscles and reduce tension, further alleviating numbness. By promoting blood circulation and reducing muscle spasms, Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils can help to alleviate numbness in the hands.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat and add the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves. Let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool. Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl.
Transfer the liquid to a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil such as jojoba oil.
Store the bottle in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the bottle daily to help the oil extract from the Rosmarinus officinalis leaves.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains limonene, menthone, and menthyl acetate as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate numbness in hands by reducing pain and swelling. Menthyl acetate also has a vasodilatory effect, which can increase blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and relief from numbness. The antispasmodic properties of menthone help relax muscles and calm nerve endings, further reducing numbness and discomfort. By applying Mentha x piperita essential oils topically, the bioactive constituents can be absorbed through the skin, providing localized relief from numbness in the hands.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh peppermint leaves (Mentha x piperita).
Wash the leaves in cold water, then dry them with a towel.
Use a steam distiller or a double boiler to heat 2 cups of water.
Add the peppermint leaves to the distiller or double boiler and let the steam extract the oil for 2-3 hours.
Strain the mixture and separate the oil from the water. Store the essential oil in a dark glass bottle.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains linalool and linalyl acetate, which are known for their analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties. These properties help to reduce numbness in the hands by alleviating pain and inflammation. Linalool, in particular, has a vasodilatory effect, which increases blood flow to the affected area and promotes healing. The antispasmodic properties of linalyl acetate also help to relax muscles and reduce tension, which can contribute to numbness in the hands. By reducing pain, inflammation, and muscle tension, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can help to alleviate numbness in the hands.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water in the saucepan over low heat until it starts to simmer.
Add the Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the saucepan and let them steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Let the liquid sit at room temperature for 24 hours to allow the essential oil to separate and rise to the surface.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils contains thymol and carvacrol, which are two main bioactive constituents that help with numbness in hands. Thymol has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling and pain, while carvacrol helps to increase blood flow to the affected area. Thymus vulgaris essential oils also has antioxidant properties that protect against cell damage and promote healing. This essential oil can also help to reduce muscle spasms and stiffness, which can contribute to numbness in the hands. When applied topically, thymus vulgaris essential oils can help to restore sensation and relieve numbness in the hands.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves and flowers. Wash them with water to remove dirt.
Combine the leaves and flowers in a blender. Blend until you get a smooth paste.
Transfer the paste to a saucepan and add 2 cups of water. Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes.
Remove the saucepan from heat and let it cool. Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl.
Transfer the strained liquid to a dark glass bottle. Store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the oil on your hands to relieve numbness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis essential oils contains several bioactive constituents, including carnosic acid, ursolic acid, and rosmarinic acid. These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to reduce swelling and promote blood flow to the hands. The anti-inflammatory properties of carnosic acid and ursolic acid can also help to reduce numbness and tingling in the hands by reducing inflammation in the nerves. Rosmarinic acid's antioxidant properties can help to protect the nerves from damage and promote healthy nerve function. By reducing inflammation and promoting healthy nerve function, Salvia officinalis essential oils may help to alleviate numbness in the hands.
remedies.image_alt: Salvia officinalis herbal essential oils for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Salvia officinalis leaves, a clean glass jar, and a carrier oil like coconut or olive oil.
Chop 1/4 cup of the Salvia leaves and place them in the glass jar.
Pour 1 cup of the carrier oil over the chopped leaves and stir well.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day to help the oil extract from the leaves.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a new glass bottle and discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains a compound called gingerol, which is responsible for its therapeutic properties. Gingerol has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which help reduce pain and swelling in the hands. The warming effect of gingerol increases blood flow to the affected area, promoting healing and relaxation of muscles. The essential oils also contain zingiberene and bisabolol, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm the nervous system. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, Zingiber officinale essential oils can help alleviate numbness in the hands.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for numbness in hands
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Zingiber officinale (Ginger) roots and 2 cups of carrier oil (like Coconut or Jojoba oil).
Chop the ginger roots into small pieces and add them to a clean glass jar with a lid. Pour the carrier oil over the ginger.
Close the jar and store it in a cool, dark place. Let it sit for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2-3 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass jar. Discard the ginger solids.
Add a few drops of Vitamin E oil (as a preservative) to the extracted oil and store it in a dark glass bottle. Use 5-7 drops of the oil to help numbness in hands.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help prevent numbness in hands from forming. Certain herbs like ginkgo biloba and ginger have anti-inflammatory properties that can improve blood flow to the hands. This can help reduce the risk of numbness caused by reduced blood circulation. Some people find that drinking these teas regularly helps keep their hands feeling warm and comfortable.
faq_safe: Consuming herbal teas for numbness in hands daily may cause more harm than good. Some herbs can interact with medications or worsen underlying conditions, especially if taken regularly. Start with small amounts to gauge your body's response, and consider switching to herbal teas that are specifically designed to promote circulation and nerve health.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas for numbness in hands can start to show results within a few weeks. Some people notice a difference in a week or two, while others may take a month or more to feel relief. It's essential to be patient and consistent with your tea consumption to see noticeable improvements in hand numbness.
faq_time: If you experience numbness in your hands and want to try herbal tea, morning or early afternoon might be a good time to drink it. This allows the herbs to circulate in your body throughout the day, potentially helping to alleviate numbness.
faq_interaction: Herbal teas can interact with medications for numbness in hands. Certain herbs like ginkgo biloba and St. John's Wort can increase blood thinning effects, which may worsen numbness. Other herbs like valerian root and passionflower can enhance sedative effects, making numbness symptoms feel worse. It's best to be cautious when drinking herbal teas with these medications.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's best to be cautious with herbal teas, especially if you're looking to relieve numbness in your hands. Some herbs, like peppermint and ginger, are generally safe, but others, like ginseng and blue cohosh, may not be. It's also crucial to check the tea's ingredients and ensure they're free from added caffeine or other stimulants.
faq_breastfeeding: Herbal teas for numbness in hands while breastfeeding are generally safe, but be cautious with strong herbs like ginger and peppermint. These can pass into breast milk and affect your baby. Opt for milder options like chamomile or lemon balm, which are considered safe for breastfeeding mothers.
